summary: In this paper, Dorret I. Boomsma and colleagues provide an update on the Netherlands Twin Register (NTR). The authors detail how the NTR has expanded beyond the classical twin design to include extended family members (parents, siblings, spouses, and offspring) and large-scale biobanking, enabling more complex genetic epidemiological studies and the testing of core assumptions in twin research.

principles.statement: The traditional twin design should be extended to include complex family relationships.
principles.rationale: Including parents, siblings, spouses, and offspring allows for more complex genetic epidemiology and heritability studies, and helps track dynamic social relationships over time.
principles.source_quote: The NTR has gone beyond the traditional twin design by including family relationships other than MZ and DZ twins in the register.

principles.statement: Combine longitudinal phenotyping with large-scale DNA typing to understand complex traits.
principles.rationale: This combination is necessary to elucidate the underlying genetic mechanisms that cause variation in health, physical, behavioral, and psychological characteristics.
principles.source_quote: The combination of longitudinal phenotyping with large-scale DNA typing hopefully will elucidate the underlying genetic mechanisms that cause variation in a wide range of health, physical, behavioral and psychological characteristics.

principles.statement: Model twin data simultaneously with sibling data to test the assumptions of the twin method.
principles.rationale: Comparing twins to non-twin siblings allows researchers to explicitly test whether twin results can be generalized to the singleton population and whether a special "twin environment" exists.
principles.source_quote: To test some of the assumptions underlying the twin method, data from twins can be modeled simultaneously with data from their siblings.

frameworks.name: Monozygotic Discordant (MZD) Design
frameworks.when_to_apply: When investigating the environmental pathways that mediate differences in specific traits or disorders, while perfectly controlling for genetic factors.
steps: Identify monozygotic (MZ) twin pairs who are discordant for a specific phenotype (e.g., depression, attention problems).
Conduct experimental or observational studies (such as fMRI) on these pairs.
Analyze the intra-pair differences to isolate environmental pathways leading to the disorder.
frameworks.source_quote: The MZ discordant (MZD) design is a powerful method to look at environmental pathways that mediate differences within MZ pairs.

frameworks.name: Extended Twin-Family Design
frameworks.when_to_apply: When conducting genetic epidemiology studies that require testing the assumptions of the classical twin method or exploring complex familial transmission.
steps: Recruit a large cohort of twins.
Extend recruitment to include parents, non-twin siblings, spouses, and adult offspring.
Collect longitudinal phenotype and genotype data across these different family relations.
Model the data simultaneously to correct for dependencies and test genetic/environmental assumptions.
frameworks.source_quote: The NTR combines the classical twin design with extended and more complex family designs to genetic epidemiology and heritability studies.

mental_models.name: The Twin-Sibling Comparison
mental_models.description: A methodological lens used to validate twin research by comparing twins to their non-twin siblings at similar ages. This ensures that findings from twins can be generalized to the broader singleton population and checks for unique environmental influences specific only to twins.
mental_models.example: Selecting an earlier survey of older siblings and a later survey for younger twins to compare their sensation-seeking behavior at the exact same age.

signature_quotes.text: The NTR has gone beyond the traditional twin design by including family relationships other than MZ and DZ twins in the register.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the evolution and restructuring of the Netherlands Twin Register database.

signature_quotes.text: The combination of longitudinal phenotyping with large-scale DNA typing hopefully will elucidate the underlying genetic mechanisms that cause variation in a wide range of health, physical, behavioral and psychological characteristics.
signature_quotes.context: Concluding remarks on the future potential of the NTR's methodology.

signature_quotes.text: The MZ discordant (MZD) design is a powerful method to look at environmental pathways that mediate differences within MZ pairs.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how large databases of well-phenotyped twins enable new quasi-experimental designs.

anti_patterns.description: Relying solely on the classical twin design without testing its underlying assumptions about generalizability.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It assumes without proof that twin results generalize to singletons and that there is no special twin environment. Including non-twin siblings allows for explicit tests of these assumptions.
